_meta.description: Schema for .code-review-manifest.json — the incremental-review tracking file written and read by /code-review. Referenced by /code-review Step 2 (Load Manifest) and Step 10 (Manifest Update).

lastRun.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent /code-review invocation that wrote this manifest.

charter.description: Charter state captured at last review — used to detect charter drift that forces re-review of all files.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hash of CHARTER.md content at last review. Prefixed with 'sha256:'. Mismatch with current charter hash forces re-review of every file.

files.description: Map from relative file path to per-file review state. Keys are paths relative to project root.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hash of file content at last review. Prefixed with 'sha256:'. Mismatch with current file hash queues the file for re-review.

status.description: Review status — see statusValues block for semantics.

reviewedAt.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent review of this file.

findingCount.description: Count of non-info findings recorded at last review. 0 when status is 'approved'.

findings.description: Findings recorded at last review. Each finding is an object with file path, line range, category, severity, description, recommendation.

issueRefs.description: Issue numbers (e.g., 1234) created from this file's findings via /bug or /enhancement.

domains.description: Domain IDs (e.g., 'security', 'accessibility') applied to this file via --with. Merged across runs — not replaced.

statusValues.pending: Never reviewed — queued on next run.
statusValues.approved: Reviewed with no findings. Skipped on incremental runs when contentHash matches.
statusValues.flagged: Reviewed and has findings. Skipped on incremental runs when contentHash matches (flagged-unchanged means already-reported issues still open).
statusValues.deferred: User skipped this file. Skipped on all incremental runs regardless of hash.

notes: First-run behavior: if .code-review-manifest.json does not exist at project root, /code-review creates an empty manifest ({version: 1, lastRun: <today>, charter: {contentHash: <current>}, files: {}}) and proceeds as if every discovered file is new.
Malformed-manifest fallback: if the file exists but fails to parse as JSON, /code-review warns and continues as if --full was passed.

Charter-hash re-review trigger: if charter.contentHash in the manifest differs from the current CHARTER.md hash, every discovered file is queued for re-review (regardless of per-file contentHash match).

Domain merging (Step 10 rule 5): when --with <domains> is active, the per-file domains array merges new domains with existing ones — it does not replace. This lets repeat runs with different domains accumulate coverage.
